description: A V8 sandbox bypass vulnerability caused by an asyncness inconsistency in BytecodeGenerator. The root cause is in BytecodeGenerator::BuildAsyncReturn(), which unconditionally calls generator_object() (returning the incoming_new_target_or_generator_ register) without verifying that the function is actually a resumable/async function. This register is only initialized in AllocateTopLevelRegisters() when IsResumableFunction(function_kind()) is true. The inconsistency is triggered by a race condition: an attacker corrupts Script::flags to set is_repl_mode=1 while Compiler::CollectSourcePositions() is re-parsing the function (e.g., triggered via RenderCallSite() during exception handling). With is_repl_mode=1, the parser takes the ParseREPLProgram() path, which unconditionally appends a NewAsyncReturnStatement() to the script body. However, the bytecode generator uses function_kind() from the FunctionLiteral (which reflects the original non-async kind) to decide control flow structure, so incoming_new_target_or_generator_ is never allocated and remains at its default-constructed invalid state (kInvalidIndex = kMaxInt). When VisitReturnStatement() processes the async return, it calls BuildAsyncReturn(), which invokes BytecodeArrayBuilder::MoveRegister() with generator_object() holding kInvalidIndex. BytecodeRegisterOptimizer::GetRegisterInfoTableIndex() then computes an index of approximately 0x80000008, causing GetRegisterInfo() to access register_info_table_ at a ~-16 GiB offset, resulting in an out-of-bounds read/write that can be leveraged for controlled address write and V8 sandbox escape.

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6192621 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
